# Blueprint v0.3.2 — uninstaller prompts about user data

Single fix for the uninstall UX gap surfaced during testing.

## What changed

The Windows uninstaller now asks **"Also delete your Blueprint user
data?"** before tearing down. Default is No (safe for upgrades and
reinstalls), but explicit Yes wipes `%USERPROFILE%\.blueprint\` so
the next install gets a true first-launch experience.

This matches the convention every well-behaved Windows installer
follows — Chrome, VS Code, etc. all ask before deleting per-user
state on uninstall.

## Why this matters

Before v0.3.2:
- "Uninstall" only removed the binaries from Program Files
- `~/.blueprint/` (models, calibrations, configs, the
  `.first-run-done` marker, etc.) survived
- Reinstalling skipped the welcome wizard and reported old models
  as "already on disk" — confusing for anyone trying to test the
  first-launch experience

After v0.3.2:
- Uninstall prompts you
- **No** → app gone, your data preserved (the upgrade case)
- **Yes** → app AND data both gone; next install is a true fresh
  start

## How to test

1. Install v0.3.2 (any path — installer over v0.3.1 works; data
   preserved through the upgrade)
2. From Windows Settings → Apps → Blueprint → Uninstall
3. The new prompt appears mid-uninstall

`/SD IDNO` in the NSIS script means unattended / silent uninstalls
default to **No**, so automation pipelines won't accidentally
delete data they didn't intend to.

## Other platforms

macOS / Linux don't have an installer-driven uninstall flow (you
drag .app to Trash, or `rm` the binary), so there's no equivalent
prompt to add there. The Maintain tab's **Reset Blueprint data**
button in the app itself remains the cross-platform path for users
who want to wipe their data without uninstalling.
